revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ayamezuka Kofun]][[ja:菖蒲塚古墳]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Nishikan-ku, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Ayamezuka Kofun | native_name = 菖蒲塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ayamezuka Kofun, Niigata, Niigata, Japan, August 2022.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ayamezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|46|00|N|138|51|57|E|display=inline, title|qq=}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nishikan-ku, Niigata|en|Nishikan-ku, Niigata|qid=Q711756|lt=Nishikan-ku, Niigata|1=Nishikan-ku, Niigata}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ayamezuka Kofun'''|菖蒲塚古墳|||qq=}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Takenomachi neighborhood of {{ill|Nishikan-ku, Niigata|en|Nishikan-ku, Niigata|qid=Q711756|lt=Nishikan-ku, Niigata|1=Nishikan-ku, Niigata}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1938.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216239/2 |title=菖蒲塚古墳穴|trans-title=Ayamezuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Ayamezuka Kofun is located on the tip of a plateau on the east foot of Mount Kakuda, a 482-meter hill in far western Niigata city, within the grounds of a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|qid=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} called Kanzen-ji. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus has a total length of 53 meters, making it one of the largest in Niigata Prefecture, and marking the northern limit at which keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' have been found on the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|qid=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} coast. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 33 meters and height of three meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 18 meters and height of two meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The tumulus was surrounded by circumferential moat, with dirt from the moat used to partly construct the tumulus. The internal structure of the ''kofun'' is not clear, and it appears that it did not have a stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], and as the tomb has been robbed in antiquity, the amount of surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} is minimal. The tomb was opened in 1812 during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, at which time a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]] with a pattern of dragons was discovered. This mirror is 22.7 cm in diameter and is a Niigata Prefectural Important Cultural Asset. Other known artifacts include one jade ''[[magatama]]'' and seven cylindrical beads, and it was recorded in Edo period records thats some iron objects had been discovered. From these items, it is estimated that the tumuli dates from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> During the Edo period, local folklore attributed the tomb to the wife of {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimasa|en|Minamoto no Yorimasa|qid=Q164948|lt=Minamoto no Yorimasa|1=Minamoto no Yorimasa}}, Ayame Gozen, hence the ''kofun'''s name. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] again in 2002 to 2003, during which time a number of pottery shards were found from the moat area. A number of the artifacts recovered from the tumulus are at the {{ill|Niigata City History Museum|en|Niigata City History Museum|qid=Q11502895|lt=Niigata City History Museum|1=Niigata City History Museum}} and are collectively designated an Important Cultural Property of Niigata Prefecture. There is another tumulus, the {{nihongo|'''Hayatozuka Kofun'''|隼人塚古墳|||qq=}}, a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}}) with a diameter of 21 meters, located a short distance from the Ayamezuka Kofun. Also on the same plateau, 500 meters to the west, is the Minami-Akasaka Site, which contains traces of a settlement which was contemporary to the building of these two tumuli. {{ill|Zoku-Jōmon period|en|Zoku-Jōmon period|qid=Q11607084|lt=Zoku-Jōmon period|1=Zoku-Jōmon period}} pottery from either [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] or the northern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} have been found at this site, indicating that at least a portion of its inhabitants were either from northern Japan, or had extensive interaction with the northern peoples. As the settlement and the ''kofun'' are contemporary, it is likely that the chieftains of the settlement was the builders of these tumuli. The Ayamezuka Kofun was used as a {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|qid=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound|1=Sutra mound}} from the end of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, and the surviving sutras recovered from the site are collectively designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property of Japan|National Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9847 |title=越後国菖蒲塚古墳経塚出土品|trans-title=|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> The tumulus is located in a rural area about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Maki Station (Niigata)|en|Maki Station (Niigata)|qid=Q6739504|lt=Maki Station|1=Maki Station (Niigata)}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Echigo Line|en|Echigo Line|qid=Q188250|lt=Echigo Line|1=Echigo Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.niigata.lg.jp/kanko/bunka/rekishi/maizobunka/shiseki/ayameduka.html Niigata city tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
<!-- {{URL|example.com|qq=}} --> [[en:Ayamezuka Kofun]]
{{wikidata link|Q11619951}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:新潟県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:新潟県の古墳]] [[Category:西蒲区の歴史]]
